summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation released the provisional Consumer Price Index (CPI) for June 2026 (base 2024=100), reporting all‑India retail inflation of 4.38% year‑on‑year and food inflation of 5.32% year‑on‑year.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- Press release dated 13 July 2026, 4:00 PM (PIB Delhi).  
- Provisional all‑India CPI inflation (June 2026 over June 2025): 4.38%; rural 4.74%, urban 3.92%.  
- Provisional CFPI (food) inflation: 5.32%; rural 5.45%, urban 5.09%.  
- Housing inflation (June 2026): 2.10% overall; rural 2.66%, urban 1.90%.  
- Index levels (June 2026 provisional): CPI General – rural 107.24, urban 106.69, combined 107.00; CFPI – rural 106.82, urban 107.72, combined 107.15.  
- Comparison with May 2026 (final): CPI General inflation was 3.93% (combined) in May; CFPI inflation was 4.78% (combined) in May.  
- Top 5 low‑inflation items (June 2026, combined): Potato –20.34% (wt 0.7549), Peas –9.67% (wt 0.1254), Motor car & jeep –6.89% (wt 0.4665), Cumin (Jeera) –3.75% (wt 0.3655), Motor cycle & scooter –3.49% (wt 0.6581).  
- Top 5 high‑inflation items (June 2026, combined): Silver Jewellery 133.21% (wt 0.3127), Ginger 50.41% (wt 0.2556), Gold/Diamond/Platinum Jewellery 36.82% (wt 0.6230), Tomato 31.92% (wt 0.4961), Raisin (kishmish) & monacca 20.52% (wt 0.1639).  
- Price data collected from 1,407 urban markets (including online) and 1,465 villages; response rate June 2026 was 100% in both rural and urban areas.  
- Next CPI release (July 2026) scheduled for 12 August 2026 (Wednesday) or the next working day if a holiday.  
- State‑ and item‑level indices for June 2026 available for download at https://esankhyiki.mospi.gov.in/ (caution advised for thin samples).

headline: Vice-President briefed on National Cadet Corps and Sainik Schools by Ministry of Defence
fullText:
Vice President's Secretariat

Vice-President briefed on National Cadet Corps and Sainik Schools by Ministry of Defence

Vice-President calls for greater State participation to strengthen NCC and expand Sainik Schools

13 JUL 2026 3:32PM by PIB Delhi

The Vice-President of India, Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan, was today briefed on the National Cadet Corps (NCC) and Sainik Schools by Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth, along with senior officers of the Ministry of Defence, at Uprashtrapati Bhavan. The presentations covered the growth of the NCC into a nearly 20-lakh-strong youth organisation, its training and nation-building initiatives, as well as the functioning and expansion of Sainik Schools, including the roadmap for strengthening the network across the country.

Vice President Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan was briefed on the National Cadet Corps (NCC) and Sainik Schools by Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth, along with senior officers of the Ministry of Defence today at Uprashtrapati Bhavan.

Appreciating the NCC's contribution to… pic.twitter.com/l29yQZtQdX

— Vice-President of India (@VPIndia) July 13, 2026

Appreciating the NCC's contribution to nation-building, the Vice-President described the Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat Camps as an excellent initiative for strengthening national integration. He also lauded the NCC's community service initiatives, including the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan, and welcomed the establishment of the NCC Alumni Association. Emphasising that strengthening the NCC is a shared national responsibility, Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan called for greater infrastructure development, enhanced coordination with State Governments on issues such as land and training infrastructure, and assured all possible support in engaging with the States.

The Vice-President was also briefed on the functioning and expansion of Sainik Schools, including the existing and Public-Private Partnership (PPP) models. Suggesting that the growing demand among students and parents be addressed, Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan observed that each State could aim to have three to five Sainik Schools. He also proposed exploring the possibility of increasing the intake capacity of existing Sainik Schools.
